brief_document.title: Type-safety verification and the end-to-end example workflow
brief_document.purpose: Prove the cluster's defining property — compile-time type safety — and demonstrate the whole surface composing. Assert representative invalid compositions are rejected by gleam build, and write a typed end-to-end example workflow exercising run/now/sleep/receive/spawn/all that compiles and passes under the harness.
brief_document.task: Add type-safety verification: document representative invalid compositions (wrong activity input type, wrong signal payload type, wrong query return type) as compile-fail expectations and verify they are rejected by gleam build (e.g. via a documented compile-fail fixture set or an inline negative-test approach). Write an end-to-end typed example workflow that uses run, now, sleep, receive, spawn, and all, and a harness test that drives it to a typed result.

requirements.title: Compile-time type-safety verification
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL document representative invalid compositions as compile-fail expectations and verify they are rejected by gleam build: a wrong activity input type, a wrong signal payload type, and a wrong query return type.
acceptance: At least three invalid compositions are captured as standalone negative fixtures under test/compile_fail/ (each a complete .gleam snippet that must FAIL to type-check), kept out of the package build so the package itself still compiles: (a) passing a value of the wrong type as an activity input, (b) receiving a SignalRef(a) into a context expecting payload b, (c) a query handler whose returned type does not match its registered type.

A committed checker script (test/compile_fail/check.sh) drives the verification: for each negative fixture it builds an ephemeral throwaway Gleam project depending on aion_flow, runs gleam check, and asserts a non-zero exit whose output contains the documented type-mismatch (the expected error substring is recorded alongside each fixture); for each fixture it also checks that the positive counterpart type-checks clean.
The approach is reproducible by a reviewer running check.sh — the static guarantee is demonstrated by an actual failing gleam check, not merely asserted in prose.

requirements.title: End-to-end typed example workflow
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide an end-to-end typed example workflow that exercises run, now, sleep, receive, spawn, and all, compiling and passing under the test harness.
acceptance: The example is a plain Gleam workflow function fn(input) -> Result(output, error) registered with workflow.define, using typed activities (run), workflow.now, a sleep, a typed receive, a child spawn, and an all fan-out — demonstrating the full surface composes with static types.
A harness test (AF-011) drives the example: mocks its activities and child, advances the clock past its sleep, sends its signal, and asserts the typed output.
The example doubles as the canonical documentation snippet for the package (referenced from aion_flow.gleam's module doc).

boundaries: SHALL NOT add new primitives — this brief verifies and demonstrates the surface built in AF-001 through AF-011.
SHALL NOT require a running engine — the example runs under the pure-Gleam harness.
SHALL NOT weaken any type signature to make the example compile; if a composition needs a type that does not exist, that is a gap to raise, not to paper over.
verification: gleam build passes for the package and the positive example.
gleam test passes for the end-to-end example under the harness.
The documented invalid compositions are confirmed rejected by gleam build; the positive counterparts compile.
No panic/todo; no type signature weakened to force compilation.

checklist.text: Activity, SignalRef, query handler, and ChildHandle types are static type parameters; representative invalid compositions (wrong activity input type, wrong signal payload type, wrong query return type) are documented as compile-fail and verified to be rejected by gleam build.
An end-to-end typed example workflow exercises run/now/sleep/receive/spawn/all and compiles, demonstrating the full surface composes with static types.

stories.text: As a workflow author, I want activity inputs and outputs to be statically typed so that passing the wrong value to an activity is a compile error, not a runtime failure three days into a long-running workflow.

constraints.text: Pure Gleam package. No Rust, no engine code, no beamr dependency inside aion_flow. It publishes to Hex standalone (gleam_stdlib is its only runtime dependency, plus gleam_json for the default JSON codecs).
Every @external(erlang, ...) declaration lives in the single internal module aion/internal/ffi. No author-facing module declares an @external (per D1).

Module files contain logic; the package root and any mod-equivalent re-export aggregator stay declarations/re-exports only. No god files — split before a module exceeds ~500 lines of code (excluding comments/whitespace).

No partial implementations. Every public function is complete and total: no panic, no todo, no assert-as-control-flow; failure paths return typed Result errors. Gleam has no exceptions for control flow — every fallible binding returns Result.

Type safety is enforced, not advisory: activity input/output, signal payload, query return, and child input/output types are static type parameters; an invalid composition fails `gleam build`. A type-safety test brief asserts representative invalid compositions are rejected (compile-fail expectations documented).

Determinism: the SDK exposes workflow.now/workflow.random bound to AD's determinism context and exposes NO binding to a wall clock or entropy source. Workflow-code documentation states the prohibition explicitly.

The recorded/side-effectful boundary is structural: the only side-effect dispatch the SDK offers is workflow.run(activity) (and all/race/map over activities). There is no generic side_effect(fn) escape hatch (per D4).

No hardcoded defaults for configurable values: no default RetryPolicy, no default timeout, no default backoff numbers baked into the SDK. An activity without a retry decorator runs once; the author supplies all policy values (CLAUDE.md no-assumed-defaults).
User data crosses the boundary only through a Codec(a); a decode failure is a typed DecodeError, never a panic. No primitive silently swallows a decode error.

aion_flow does not implement engine mechanics (timer firing, signal routing, replay) — those are AE/AD/AT, reached via @external. It does not author NIFs in Rust — that is AN. It is not packaged into .aion — that is AP. The cluster owns only the Gleam API surface and its binding declarations.
All acceptance uses Gleam tooling: gleam build, gleam check, gleam format --check, gleam test must all pass clean. The test harness (aion/testing) runs under gleam test with no external services.

resolved_context.intention: Define aion_flow, the Gleam package (published to Hex) that workflow authors import to write durable workflows. It is the human-facing surface of the entire engine: a workflow author writes a plain, statically-typed Gleam function — decisions, loops, data transformation — and reaches the outside world only through this SDK's activity, signal, query, timer, and child-workflow primitives. The package is pure Gleam with no engine code inside it; every primitive is a thin @external(erlang) binding that resolves at runtime, when beamr loads the compiled workflow and the engine has registered the matching NIFs (AE-004), to the engine-side mechanics in AE/AD/AT. The defining property is compile-time type safety: activity inputs and outputs, signal payloads, and query returns are all statically typed, so an invalid composition — passing a String where an activity expects an Order, awaiting a signal of the wrong type, returning the wrong type from a query handler — fails at `gleam build`, not three days into a long-running production workflow. This is the concrete advantage over Temporal's SDKs (Go weakly typed, TypeScript better but runtime-checked). The package also ships aion/testing, the Temporal-equivalent test harness: simulate time so a month-long sleep resolves instantly, mock activities to return canned results, and assert that a workflow replays to the same state — all in plain gleam test, with no engine, no beamr, and no store.
